description: US B-2 Spirit stealth bombers struck the IRGC Aerospace Force headquarters compound in northern Tehran with GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrators, destroying underground command bunkers. At least 40 senior IRGC officers were killed including two brigadier generals.

Simultaneous cruise missile salvo targeted the Supreme Leader's residential and administrative complex in the Niavaran district of northern Tehran. Iranian state media confirmed significant structural damage but claimed Khamenei was relocated hours prior.

JASSM-ER cruise missiles launched from B-1B Lancers struck the Iranian Ministry of Defense and Armed Forces Logistics compound, collapsing multiple buildings and destroying the central communications hub.

Social media reports and satellite imagery suggest a military logistics depot adjacent to Imam Khomeini International Airport was struck overnight. Secondary explosions were visible for several hours, suggesting ammunition stores were hit.

Israeli F-35I Adir jets penetrated Iranian airspace and delivered precision-guided munitions on the Isfahan Nuclear Technology Center and uranium conversion facility. The strike destroyed centrifuge assembly halls and contaminated surrounding areas with low-level radioactive material.

US Navy Tomahawk cruise missiles launched from destroyers in the Arabian Sea struck the Isfahan power generation station supplying electricity to remaining nuclear infrastructure. The strike caused widespread blackouts across Isfahan province.

Multiple waves of Israeli F-35I and F-15I aircraft delivered GBU-57 bunker busters on the deeply buried Natanz uranium enrichment facility, the crown jewel of Operation Rising Lion. IAEA later confirmed the underground centrifuge halls suffered catastrophic structural collapse.

The hardened Fordow Fuel Enrichment Plant, buried deep inside a mountain near Qom, was struck by Israeli penetrator munitions in a sustained 45-minute assault. While full destruction was not achieved, all access tunnels were collapsed and the facility rendered inoperable.

US Navy Tomahawk missiles struck the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant complex, targeting the reactor's cooling systems and auxiliary buildings. Iran accused the US of risking a nuclear catastrophe. IAEA emergency teams were dispatched to assess radiological contamination.

Massive US air and naval strike on the IRIN (Islamic Republic of Iran Navy) main base at Bandar Abbas. B-2 bombers and carrier-based F/A-18s destroyed drydocks, missile boat pens, and the naval headquarters building. Three Iranian frigates were sunk at their moorings.

Israeli Air Force F-15I Ra'am aircraft struck Shahid Doran Airbase near Shiraz, targeting hardened aircraft shelters housing Iranian Su-35 fighters recently delivered by Russia. At least 6 aircraft were destroyed on the ground along with radar installations.
casualties: 18 air force personnel killed, 6 aircraft destroyed

description: US cruise missiles struck the IRGC Ground Forces regional headquarters in Tabriz, destroying the main command building and adjacent barracks. The strike was part of a broader campaign to degrade IRGC command and control across northwestern Iran.

Unconfirmed reports from Iranian social media indicate explosions near a suspected IRGC Quds Force training and logistics facility on the outskirts of Mashhad. The Pentagon declined to comment. Satellite imagery showed burn scars consistent with cruise missile impacts.

US strikes targeted the IRGC 92nd Armored Division base near Ahvaz in Khuzestan province, destroying vehicle depots, fuel storage, and a mobile missile launcher staging area. Secondary explosions continued for hours after the initial strike.

A US JASSM-ER missile struck a building in Minab that the Pentagon identified as a suspected IRGC missile logistics warehouse. The building was in fact a girls' secondary school in session. Between 85 and 180 schoolgirls aged 12-17 were killed in the deadliest single incident of the war, sparking global outrage and mass protests.

Israeli strikes hit the Parchin military research complex southeast of Tehran, long suspected of housing nuclear weapons research facilities. Massive explosions were reported as underground chambers containing explosive testing equipment were destroyed.

Residents in the industrial city of Karaj reported multiple explosions overnight near a centrifuge component manufacturing facility previously linked to Iran's nuclear program. Israel did not confirm the strike but satellite imagery showed significant damage to factory buildings.

Multiple explosions reported in the outskirts of the holy city of Qom near suspected IRGC underground missile storage tunnels. Iran condemned the strikes near religious sites. The US neither confirmed nor denied the reports.

The IR-40 heavy water reactor at Arak, capable of producing weapons-grade plutonium, was struck by Israeli F-35I jets during the opening hours of Operation Rising Lion. The reactor vessel was breached and cooling systems destroyed, rendering the facility permanently inoperable.

US B-2 bombers struck a hardened underground IRGC ballistic missile base near Dezful in Khuzestan province, targeting a facility believed to house Shahab-3 and Emad medium-range ballistic missiles. Massive secondary explosions indicated missile propellant detonation.

Iran launched Operation True Promise 3 in retaliation for Operation Rising Lion, firing over 300 ballistic missiles and cruise missiles at Israel. Several Emad and Shahab-3 missiles penetrated Iron Dome and Arrow-3 defenses, impacting near the Kirya military headquarters compound in central Tel Aviv. Significant structural damage to surrounding buildings was reported.

Iranian ballistic missile debris and at least one warhead reportedly impacted near Ben Gurion International Airport, forcing a complete shutdown of Israel's main aviation hub. Damage to runway infrastructure was reported but not officially confirmed by Israeli authorities.

Hezbollah launched a sustained barrage of Fajr-5 and Zelzal heavy rockets at Haifa, striking the port area and nearby oil refineries. A direct hit on a fuel storage tank triggered a massive fire that burned for three days. Haifa port operations were halted indefinitely.

In the largest single missile attack of the war, Iran launched 9 successive waves of ballistic missiles at Jerusalem over a 4-hour period, overwhelming Iron Dome and David's Sling batteries. Multiple impacts were recorded across the city, including near the Old City walls. International condemnation was swift from all parties.

Iranian Shahab-3 missiles targeted Nevatim Airbase south of Beer Sheva, home to Israel's F-35I Adir fleet, during Operation True Promise 3. While most missiles were intercepted by Arrow-3, two warheads impacted the base perimeter, damaging taxiways and a maintenance hangar.

Unconfirmed reports indicate Iranian Khorramshahr missiles were directed at the Negev Nuclear Research Center near Dimona. Israeli censors imposed a full blackout on reporting. Seismic sensors detected impacts in the area but Israel stated all inbound threats were neutralized.

A combined drone and ballistic missile swarm targeted Ramat David Airbase in the Jezreel Valley during Operation True Promise 3. Over 50 Shahed-136 drones saturated air defenses while Fateh-110 missiles struck the runway and flight line, destroying 3 F-16I aircraft in their shelters.

An Iranian Emad ballistic missile warhead struck a residential apartment block in the city of Beit Shemesh after evading Iron Dome interceptors. The four-story building collapsed entirely, killing nine civilians including three children. Rescue operations continued for 36 hours.
source: IDF Home Front Command / Israeli Media

description: Residents of Bat Yam, south of Tel Aviv, reported loud explosions and missile debris falling in a commercial district. Israeli military censors restricted reporting but social media videos showed shattered storefronts and car fires consistent with interceptor debris impacts.

During Operation True Promise 3, missile impacts were reported near the Weizmann Institute of Science in Rehovot. Social media footage showed a large crater in an open field approximately 500 meters from the campus. No structural damage to the institute was confirmed.

Iranian cruise missiles struck Ashdod Port, Israel's largest cargo port, hitting container yards and a grain silo. The strike was part of Iran's strategy to disrupt Israeli supply lines. Port operations were suspended and shipping routes diverted to Eilat.

Hezbollah launched a massive barrage of over 200 Fajr-5 and Katyusha rockets at the northern Israeli city of Kiryat Shmona in coordination with Iran's missile offensive. The city, already largely evacuated, suffered widespread destruction to residential buildings, schools, and infrastructure.

Iran struck Al-Udeid Air Base, the largest US military facility in the Middle East, with a salvo of Shahab-3 ballistic missiles and Paveh cruise missiles. THAAD and Patriot batteries intercepted most inbound missiles but at least 3 struck the flight line, damaging parked KC-135 tanker aircraft and a C-17 transport.

Iranian Fateh-110 short-range ballistic missiles struck Camp Arifjan, the primary US Army logistics hub in Kuwait. Strikes hit vehicle staging areas and a warehouse complex, destroying dozens of MRAPs and supply containers. Patriot interceptors neutralized 8 of 12 inbound missiles.

An Iranian Emad ballistic missile struck a US Navy logistics support area at Port Shuaiba in Kuwait, hitting a personnel quarters building during the early morning hours. Six US service members were killed in their sleep, making it the single deadliest US loss in the opening hours of Iran's retaliatory strikes.

Iranian cruise missiles and Shahed-136 drones targeted Al Dhafra Air Base near Abu Dhabi, home to US F-35A fighters and MQ-9 Reaper drones. UAE and US air defenses intercepted most threats but 2 cruise missiles struck taxiway infrastructure, temporarily halting flight operations.
source: CENTCOM Statement / UAE Ministry of Defense

description: Iranian Fateh-313 precision-guided missiles struck the vicinity of Erbil Air Base in Iraqi Kurdistan, targeting US special operations forces and intelligence facilities. Several missiles impacted the base perimeter, destroying communications equipment and a drone hangar.

In a repeat of the January 2020 strikes, Iran launched 15 Qiam-1 ballistic missiles at Al-Asad Air Base in Anbar province. US forces had partially evacuated to hardened shelters after the initial March 1 attacks, but 4 missiles struck occupied facilities, causing significant casualties.

Unconfirmed reports indicate Iranian missiles or drones targeted Naval Support Activity Bahrain in the Juffair district of Manama, headquarters of the US Fifth Fleet. Bahraini and US officials declined to comment, but local residents reported air defense activations and distant explosions overnight.

Iranian cruise missiles struck the Ras Laffan Industrial City, the world's largest LNG export terminal, causing fires at two liquefaction trains and damaging a loading berth. Global LNG prices surged 40% within hours. Qatar condemned the attack as an act of economic warfare against a neutral state.
source: Qatari Government Statement / Energy Industry Sources

description: Unverified reports circulated on social media of explosions near the Jebel Ali port complex in Dubai, the largest port in the Middle East. UAE authorities issued a terse denial, but commercial shipping satellite data showed vessels diverting away from the port for 18 hours.

Iranian Mohajer-6 armed drones struck a suspected Israeli intelligence listening post near the Azerbaijani exclave of Nakhchivan, bordering Iran and Turkey. Azerbaijan condemned the violation of its sovereignty and recalled its ambassador from Tehran. NATO member Turkey placed forces on heightened alert.
source: Azerbaijani Ministry of Defense / Turkish Media

description: Israeli Air Force conducted intensive strikes on Hezbollah command infrastructure in Beirut's Dahieh (southern suburbs), targeting underground bunkers used for coordinating rocket attacks on northern Israel. Entire city blocks were flattened in what Lebanon called a disproportionate escalation.
casualties: 65+ killed including Hezbollah commanders, 200+ civilian casualties

description: Israeli strikes hit suspected Hezbollah rocket launcher positions and weapons storage sites in and around the ancient coastal city of Tyre in southern Lebanon. A UN peacekeeping outpost was damaged in the strikes, prompting UNIFIL to evacuate remaining personnel from the area.

US Tomahawk cruise missiles and MQ-9 Reaper drone strikes targeted Houthi (Ansar Allah) senior military leadership compounds in the Yemeni capital Sana'a. The strikes killed a senior Houthi military commander responsible for coordinating anti-shipping missile attacks in the Red Sea and Bab el-Mandeb strait.
casualties: 20+ Houthi fighters killed including senior commander

description: Iranian missile struck a building in the southern Syrian city of Sweida, killing four people. The strike highlighted the expanding geographic scope of Iranian retaliatory attacks beyond traditional conflict zones.

An Iranian-made drone damaged the runway at the British RAF base at Akrotiri, Cyprus. This marked the first attack on European NATO territory in the conflict, raising fears of further escalation beyond the Middle East.

A military projectile struck a residential facility in the Al-Kharj area south of Riyadh, causing the first civilian deaths in Saudi Arabia during the conflict. The attack marked a significant escalation of Iranian strikes against Gulf states.
A hostile drone struck a desalination plant in Salman Industrial City, Bahrain, causing a fire that killed one worker. The attack on critical water infrastructure drew international condemnation.

The IDF launched wide-scale strikes targeting Tehran's energy resources and regime targets, marking a new phase in the conflict. Multiple energy infrastructure sites across the capital were hit in coordinated waves of precision strikes.

Qatar intercepted 17 ballistic missiles and 6 drones launched by Iran targeting the Doha area. All incoming projectiles were neutralized by Qatari and allied air defense systems, preventing any casualties or damage on the ground.

An Iranian ballistic missile struck a residential area in the Sitra district of Bahrain, causing significant damage to apartment buildings and injuring 32 civilians. One person was killed in the attack. The strike marked the deadliest Iranian attack on Bahraini civilian areas since the conflict began, drawing strong condemnation from the GCC.

A NATO Patriot battery stationed in southern Turkey intercepted an Iranian ballistic missile that entered Turkish airspace. The missile, believed to be a stray or deliberately provocative launch, was destroyed before reaching Turkish territory. NATO invoked Article 4 consultations, with Turkey condemning the airspace violation as a hostile act.
source: NATO Press Release / Turkish Ministry of Defense

description: US-Israeli airstrike on residential buildings in eastern Tehran killed at least 40 people. Earlier attacks on nearby oil facilities blanketed the capital in toxic smoke. One of the deadliest single attacks on civilian areas since the conflict began.
Five people killed and several injured in a US-Israeli air strike on a residential building in the city of Arak in western Iran, according to Iranian state news agency ISNA.

Strikes on Isfahan damaged multiple UNESCO World Heritage sites including Naqsh-e Jahan Square, Chehel Sotoun palace, Ali Qapu, Shah Mosque, and Teymouri Hall. International cultural organizations condemned the destruction of irreplaceable heritage.
Iranian army launched drone attack targeting an Israeli oil and gas refinery and fuel tanks in Haifa. Israeli air defenses engaged multiple incoming drones. Damage assessment ongoing.
Saudi Arabia's Defense Ministry confirmed its military intercepted and destroyed an Iranian drone east of al-Kharj governorate, south of Riyadh. No casualties or damage reported.
A building in Seef district of Manama damaged by reported Iranian drone strike. The attack on the commercial district deepened fears of Iranian targeting of civilian infrastructure in Gulf states.

Iran deploys Khoramshahr super-heavy ballistic missiles for the first time in its 37th wave of attacks. The Khoramshahr is Iran's most powerful conventional missile with a 1,800km range and 1,800kg warhead. Multiple intercepted by Arrow-3 system.

3 more commercial ships attacked by IRGC Navy forces in the Strait of Hormuz, bringing total to 14 vessels damaged or sunk since the war began. Shipping traffic down 95% from pre-war levels. 330+ vessels now at anchor.

Iranian explosive-laden boats attacked two fuel tankers (Marshall Islands-flagged Safesea Vishnu and Malta-flagged Zefyros) in Iraqi waters, setting them ablaze. 1 crew member killed, 25 evacuated. Iraq shut down oil port operations and condemned it as 'sabotage' and 'flagrant violation of sovereignty.'

Thai-flagged Mayuree Naree dry bulk vessel struck by two projectiles while transiting the Strait of Hormuz, causing fire and engine room damage. 3 crew missing (believed trapped), 20 evacuated to Oman. Japan-flagged ONE Majesty sustained minor damage 25nm NW of Ras Al Khaimah. Two more vessels also struck. Total ships attacked since war began: at least 16.

Israeli airstrike hit apartment in Aisha Bakkar area of central Beirut, a densely populated neighborhood near one of the city's biggest shopping malls. Fireball erupted on 7th-8th floors. Separately, strike on Ramlet al-Baida seafront killed at least 7 people.

Hezbollah rocket struck Moshav Haniel in central Israel, virtually destroying a home and creating a large crater. Residents survived by reaching reinforced safe room during 90-second siren. Building destroyed except for safe room.
